enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about reggae music and Rastafarianism, sharing rambling personal opinions and a jailhouse anecdote about a man who toured with Bob Marley. He covers the origins of reggae, his view that Rastafarianism has two faces (the religious/Ethiopia aspect and a stoner lifestyle religion), and claims personal identification as a Rasta.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: invented reggae and believed music could fix all the world's problems by spreading positivity
has two separate faces: the Haile Selassie/Ethiopia religious side and the stoner lifestyle side, similar to how Scientology has two faces
Rastafarianism is essentially the original stoner religion and provides a good cultural cohesive identity for that community
identifies himself as a Rasta and challenges anyone who disputes it
thinks it was a good thing
likes it, especially Bob Marley's Legend album from around 10th grade; hasn't been listening to it much lately

claims.subject: reggae music
claims.claim: Reggae music sounds relaxed because people in the tropics smoke weed and live a relaxed lifestyle.

claims.subject: Bob Marley
claims.claim: Bob Marley invented reggae music; there wasn't any reggae before him.

claims.subject: Bob Marley
claims.claim: Bob Marley believed music could fix all the world's problems by spreading happiness and positivity.

claims.subject: Bob Marley
claims.claim: Bob Marley started with 'Scott,' which was just a basic beat before evolving into reggae.

claims.subject: Rastafarianism
claims.claim: Rastafarianism is the religion where Haile Selassie was believed to be the reincarnation of Jesus Christ.

claims.subject: Haile Selassie
claims.claim: Haile Selassie was the Prince of Ethiopia.

claims.subject: Marcus Garvey
claims.claim: Marcus Garvey wanted Africans in Jamaica to move back to Africa to establish a strong new country, with Ethiopia as a prime location due to its rich history.

claims.subject: Rastafarianism
claims.claim: Rastafarianism has two aspects, similar to Scientology—one spiritual and one focused on natural living.

claims.subject: Rastafarianism
claims.claim: Rastafarianism is essentially a 'stoner religion' centered around marijuana use, natural living, and dreadlocks.

claims.subject: Rastafarianism
claims.claim: Rastafarians believe in vegetarianism, natural living, and avoiding impure foods like shellfish.

claims.subject: Snoop Dogg
claims.claim: Snoop Dogg did a good thing by embracing Rastafarian culture, growing dreadlocks, and making reggae music.

claims.subject: Rastafarianism
claims.claim: Rastafarianism is a tribal and raw belief system that can unify people, especially Black communities.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle first listened to Bob Marley in 10th grade and it made him want to smoke weed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle would grow his hair out like a Rasta if it were more comfortable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle considers himself a Rasta and challenges anyone who says otherwise.

claims.subject: weed
claims.claim: Listening to Bob Marley and smoking weed are connected experiences.

claims.subject: weed
claims.claim: There should be a 'weed religion' to unite stoners under a shared culture.

transcript.text: Okay, so what was the outcome of your court case?
Where the fuck is that request?
I was just looking at it.
So what was the outcome of your court case probation?
Um, I assume you don't get tested.
I answered him what the outcome was.
Thoughts on reggae music and Rastafarianism?
Maybe read Wikipedia first if you don't know much about them.
I know enough without reading Wikipedia.
I know all about the Rastafarian and the reggae.
You're a reggae dude, aren't you?
It's the black people from Africa.
And then the white man came and took him to...
And that's what Buffalo Soldier is all about.
It's relaxing to live in the tropics.
And black people, I guess, are, like, relaxed.
Well, I guess maybe I don't know that much about it.
You know, let me tell you a little story here.
And he said he was walking around...
Like, in 1980, he was walking around some town.
Like, big town Detroit or something.
And some dude walked up and said,
Hey, you want to join our entourage?
And it was, like, three busloads full.
And then there was, like, some weird singer with the dreadlocks.
And playing this weird music from Jamaica.
And, yeah, it turned out to be...
Bob Marley and he toured with him for two months.
As, like, part of his entourage.
And, like, made friends with, like, Ziggy Marley.
He keeps in touch with Ziggy Marley.
And, like, you hung out with him last year.
They said that Bob Marley was all about...
Said belief that music was all about, um...
All the problems in the world could be fixed by music.
Like, by just spreading, like, happiness.
Yeah, that's what reggae music is.
I think reggae music was started by Bob Marley.
There wasn't any reggae before Bob Marley.
That was just the beat, I guess, of the tropics.
But it was, like, too uptight or whatever.
Because they smoked a bunch of weed.
Reggae was kind of like ska...
Now, Rastafarianism is officially the religion of Rastafarianism.
Is that Haile Selassie was the reincarnation of Jesus Christ.
Haile Selassie was the prince of Ethiopia.
He wanted to make a bunch of...
He wanted to make the Africans of, um...
And start, like, you know, a good African second birth great country.
And, um, so that, you know, they thought Ethiopia would be a good place because it has such rich history, you know?
It's just as rich as anywhere in the world, pretty much.
And old and, um, you know, like Jewish...
The Ark of the Covenant is there.
So, uh, and so they wanted, you know, they had the vision of Jesus becoming, um, you know, being like the second Messiah, you know, for them.
But, that didn't work out there.
The other views of the Rastafarianisms, it really doesn't have anything to do with that, you know?
It's kind of like Rastafarianism has two faces in the same way that, um, Scientology has two faces.
And Scientology has two faces in all the crazy shit that they believe about Xeno.
And then they have just the weird, simple self-help stuff with the muscle testing and the lie detector and all that, you know?
They're Scientologists who don't give a fuck about Xeno.
And the same thing with Reggae, you know?
It's like they're just two separate units that they clomped together.
Um, because the other part of Rastafarianism is that they just...
It's just people, you know, stoners.
So, it's basically, if you're going to have a religion of Rastafarianism, you could call...
I mean, if you're going to have a religion of stonerism, you know, like invent a religion of stonerism out of the air,
Instead of just inventing the religion called religion of stonerism or the marijuana religion,
What, because Rastafarianism is the original stoner religion and it came out of the stoner mentality and the stoner this and the stoner that and the stoner everything.
And so, what Snoop Doggy Dogg did, by calling him himself Snoop Lion and Grow in Dread and doing Reggae,
All those Rastafarians saying, no, you're not Rasta, can suck a big black dick.
Like, okay, if you're going to pull that bullshit, maybe I shouldn't let you come to America.
You know, America's just for us.
You can't be like, no, Rastafarian's just for us.
What, did you inherit, like, the Rasta gene or something?
You going to try and tell me I'm not Rasta?
Um, because what they believe in is all that stuff.
You know, like, I would let my hair grow out if I could.
Not my facial hair because it's physically not that comfortable.
And just be natural, you know?
Try to just be as natural as you can walk around barefoot.
Don't eat, um, shellfish and stuff because they eat, they're less pure.
Eat the nice big fish, pure fish, cleanliness, vegetables, you know, vegetarianism, that kind of stuff.
Um, that's, that's the kind of stuff that a stoner thinks about because a stoner is constantly ingesting the earth.
So then the earth says, oh, yes, more earth and more fruit.
So it's just, and it's a new religion.
But, yeah, maybe I should, yeah, I don't want to listen to Rastafarianism again on Wikipedia.
So that didn't do as good of a job.
I haven't really been listening to it that much.

When I, I first started listening to Bob Marley and, like, when I was in, like, 10th grade or something, I, like, you know, when the CDs came out, I was starting to, we got CD Bob Marley Legend and I thought it was awesome.
I was just mellow and it made me want to smoke weed, you know.
And when I smoke weed, I want to listen to Bob Marley.
It's like, it's, it's like it's the same thing.
So that's why there shouldn't be another weed or legend because now you're not, like, barfocating, bifurcating.
You want to, you want to keep it all unit.
You want to keep all the stoners together.
Stoners have a way of, like, fighting each other because it's so tribal.
You know, it's so raw, basic.
But you want to, you want to have it cohese around something.
And to have, and to have a whole bunch of culture cohese around something that was started by the black people is a good way to have, to bring the black people into, uh, into something.
